question: Is the image clearly a chess set placed on a wooden table?
Is the camera positioned from White's side at a slight overhead angle, such that White's back rank is the row nearest to the viewer?
Is the chessboard correctly oriented with a light-colored square in the bottom-right corner relative to White's perspective?
On White's back rank (the row nearest the camera), are the pieces arranged in the correct standard order from left to right: Rook, Knight, Bishop, Queen, King, Bishop, Knight, Rook?
Are the White Queen and King correctly positioned (Queen on her own color/d1, King on the opposite color/e1) without being swapped?
Is there exactly one White pawn that has moved from its starting position?
Is the moved White pawn specifically the King's Pawn (the one originally standing directly in front of the King on the e-file)?
Has the White King's Pawn advanced exactly two squares to the e4 position?
Do all other pieces (Black pieces and remaining White pieces) remain in their standard starting positions?
Is the chessboard completely free of coordinate letters (a-h) and numbers (1-8) on the borders or squares?
Is the image free of any readable text, numbers, logos, or watermarks anywhere in the scene?
Does the image exhibit high-detail realistic lighting with consistent shadows cast by the pieces?
Are the chess pieces and board free of severe structural distortions, melting effects, or geometric artifacts?
